meta.title: necro

source.publication: Section 4: Roots, Prefixes, Suffixes and Foreign Words (owner-supplied reference)
source.usage_note: Personal study only. Root, words and meanings transcribed faithfully from the source PDF; a small number of well-attested real words on the same root are included where the source list was too short to form a complete family (4 to 8 members). Parts, glosses, context sentences, mentor note, attempt and construct options are the teaching layer, authored for the Language Garden (LANGUAGE_GARDEN_BIBLE §10).

meta.reviewer_notes: Split from the source PDF’s "mort, necro" entry: mort is Latin and necro is Greek, and a plant’s root.origin_language can only hold one value, so an honest split was needed. mort is its sibling plant.

root.label: necro
root.mentor_note: necro is Greek for death, and it usually sounds a little colder than its Latin cousin, mort.

attempt.direction_prompt: A record kept of the living, or a record kept of the dead?

context_sentences: Archaeologists spent a full season mapping the necropolis before excavating a single tomb.
The city’s necropolis, silent for two thousand years, still drew more visitors than the museum.
The society’s annual necrology remembered thirty members lost over the previous year.
A single line in the journal’s necrology was the first anyone outside the family heard of his death.
The novel’s villain practises necromancy in secret, raising long buried secrets rather than corpses.
Court records from the period show at least one nobleman accused of necromancy and exiled for it.

construct_options.text: The supposed practice of communicating with the dead to see the future
A large, ancient cemetery belonging to a city
The medical study of how the body ages

context_sentences: The surgeon explained that necrosis had already set into the tissue around the wound.
Untreated, the infection can spread until necrosis forces the loss of the entire limb.

construct_options.text: The death of cells or tissue within a living body
A record or list of everyone who has recently died
A condition of being unable to fall asleep
